_comment: Labeled gold set for verify_citations.py --selftest. Each tuple is a synthetic bib entry exercising one verdict path of the verifier, resolved hermetically against recorded_responses.json (no network). Real DOIs are reused from references.bib; fabricated/unreachable DOIs are recorded as not-found / error fixtures. Mirrors the reference repo's evals/gold/citation_extraction design, specialized to economics.

schema.title: title as it would appear in the bib entry
schema.author: author field in bibtex form
schema.year: year as recorded in the bib entry
schema.note: bib note field (used for no-DOI exemptions)

groundedness_comment: Snippet cases for the prose-level groundedness gate. Each is resolved hermetically against the in-repo references.bib (no network). 'text' is a markdown fragment; 'check_author_year' mirrors the per-surface policy (skills/docs check author-year, examples are key-only); 'expected' is the verdict that must appear, or NONE when the case must produce no failure (false-positive guards).

groundedness_cases.text: Use the Oster (2019) bounding argument for selection on unobservables.
Exogenous shares (Goldsmith-Pinkham, Sorkin, Swift 2020) -- report Rotemberg weights.
The imputation estimator of Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess (2024) avoids the contamination.
Heterogeneous TWFE bias follows de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille (2020).
A robust correction was proposed by Phantomson and Vapor (2099).
See the bounding logic in Oster (2051) for the adjustment.
As shown in Table (2019), the coefficients are stable across columns.
Apply the stepdown of `romano_wolf_2005` to control the FWER.
Built on the framework of `nonexistent_method_2099` in `../../references.bib`.

Example ledger:
```text
phantom_author_2099   yes   yes
```
The ledger above is illustrative only.
Avoid raw variable names like "hh_inc_2017" in published tables.
Hjort and Poulsen (2019) study peer effects in a Kenyan factory.
A forthcoming result by Newauthor (2099) is discussed. <!-- cite-exempt: working paper, not yet in bib -->
Write keys in inline code, e.g. `` `oster_2019` ``. A raw column name like "hh_inc_2017" must never reach a published table.
Cite keys in inline code, e.g. `` `ghost_method_2099` ``, which must exist in the bibliography.
